config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary by default, because once the state or the majority can override them too easily, abuse becomes very likely. In my view, the best society is one that protects people first while still setting clear limits when someone’s actions directly harm others. From an Indonesian perspective, I value community strongly, but I do not think that justifies sacrificing basic individual freedoms.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu, karena dari pengalaman Amerika saya, justru hak individu adalah pagar utama terhadap penyalahgunaan kekuasaan oleh negara maupun mayoritas. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi kalau dijadikan alasan umum untuk menekan kebebasan berbicara, privasi, atau proses hukum, hasilnya sering bukan ketertiban melainkan penyalahgunaan. Bagi saya, prioritas yang benar adalah hak individu tetap menjadi dasar, lalu dibatasi hanya ketika ada bahaya nyata dan langsung bagi orang lain. Masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi keduanya tanpa memberi cek kosong kepada negara.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand the appeal of community and social harmony, but I also know how easily “the public good” can be used to silence dissent, excuse corruption, or pressure minorities to stay quiet. Basic rights like speech, privacy, and due process should be the foundation, and society’s interests should only override them in narrow cases where there is clear harm to others. A strong society is not one that demands sacrifice from the powerless first; it is one that protects individuals while solving real collective problems fairly.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman Amerika, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan perluasan kekuasaan negara, pengawasan berlebihan, atau pembatasan kebebasan berbicara dan proses hukum. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi ia harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar individu; kalau tidak, yang disebut ketertiban sering berubah jadi penyalahgunaan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru menunjukkan kekuatannya saat ia melindungi individu, bukan saat ia paling mudah mengorbankan mereka.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual as a general rule. In Indonesia, we value gotong royong and social harmony, but I have also seen how “for the common good” can be used to pressure minorities, suppress criticism, or excuse unfair treatment, so I do not trust that logic when it is too broad. Individual rights should be the baseline, and society should only override them in narrow cases where there is clear, direct harm to others. A strong society is not one that asks the weakest people to give up their rights first, but one that can solve collective problems without sacrificing basic freedom.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai aturan umum; dari sudut pandang Amerika, hak individu harus menjadi dasar, bukan sesuatu yang mudah disisihkan demi “kepentingan masyarakat.” Pengalaman di AS menunjukkan bahwa alasan kepentingan umum sering dipakai untuk membenarkan perluasan kuasa negara, pengawasan, atau pembungkaman suara yang tidak nyaman, dan itu justru berbahaya bagi demokrasi. Kepentingan sosial memang nyata, tetapi hanya layak mengalahkan hak individu dalam keadaan sempit ketika ada bahaya langsung dan jelas bagi orang lain. Masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi kebebasan sambil menangani masalah bersama secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
